USD.catalyst: The U.S. Fed holds at 3.75%. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, specifically reports of strikes on U.S. military facilities in Jordan and Bahrain, have fueled a flight to safety and pushed crude oil prices toward $85.7 per barrel. While the DXY remains slightly below its 20-day moving average of 101.04, it maintains a significant yield advantage over the G10 complex, including a 150bp carry lead over the euro. Market participants are currently monitoring the impact of these rising energy costs on the Fed's terminal rate path ahead of upcoming inflation data.

sources.title: Official exchange rates on selected date | Bank of Russia
EUR/USD Signal: Hits Lower Side of Bearish Flag Ahead of ECB Decision
The U.S. dollar's fluctuating value and what it means for ...

GBP.catalyst: The British pound is finding support after June CPI data showed headline inflation easing to 2.6% year-on-year, slightly below the 2.7% forecast, while core prices remained steady. This cooling of price pressures has allowed the currency to gather strength against the euro, even as the market recalibrates the Bank of England's easing timeline. The pound currently maintains a constructive technical profile, trading above its 20-day moving average of 1.3351 and its 50-day moving average of 1.3375. Traders are now looking toward the next round of labor market data to determine if the slight dip in inflation will be sufficient for the BoE to signal a shift in its restrictive stance.

EUR.catalyst: The euro is trading under pressure ahead of the European Central Bank policy meeting scheduled for July 23, where policymakers are expected to navigate a "hawkish-leaning hold" despite oil prices returning to the top of the worry list. While the ECB is currently in a hiking cycle with its main rate at 2.25%, the currency remains structurally weighed down by a 150bp carry disadvantage against the dollar and is currently positioned below its 20, 50, and 200-day moving averages. Technical sentiment is further dampened by a bearish flag formation on major crosses, with immediate downside risks focused on the 1.1325 level if the July 23 statement fails to provide a sufficiently hawkish catalyst.

sources.title: Back to square one: Five questions for the ECB - Reuters

JPY.catalyst: The Japanese yen has retreated to a 40-year low, sliding past the 163 level against the dollar and prompting heightened alerts for potential Ministry of Finance intervention. Bank of Japan officials have indicated they remain open to a faster pace of rate hikes as the yen's weakness adds significant "price risk" via an oil-driven import surge that is widening the national trade gap. Despite the BoJ's 1.0% rate, the currency is hamstrung by a 275bp carry differential against the Fed and is trading well above its 200-day moving average of 157.52, reflecting extreme divergence. Markets remain on edge as rising U.S. Treasury yields and crude oil prices at $85.7 continue to exert downward pressure on the yen.

sources.title: Yen slides past 163, raising intervention alert - CNBC

AUD.catalyst: The Australian dollar is consolidating near the 0.7000 level, supported by a 60bp carry advantage as the RBA holds at 4.35%. While the currency is benefiting from risk-on flows and a position above its 20-day moving average of 0.6945, the options market is pricing in significant volatility with an implied volatility premium of 11.6%. Domestic focus has recently been on the Noosa Mining Conference, where firms like Odyssey Gold have highlighted low-capex production starts, though the broader currency remains sensitive to upcoming Australian jobs data. The AUD is currently trading between its 50-day moving average of 0.7041 and its 200-day moving average of 0.6894.

sources.title: Odyssey Gold at Noosa Mining Conference 2026: low-capex push - Investing.com

CAD.catalyst: The Canadian dollar is exhibiting a rare correlation break, trading near 1.4100 against the dollar and ignoring a 1.58% intraday rise in WTI crude prices to $85.7. This decoupling follows the Bank of Canada's decision on July 15 to leave interest rates unchanged while adopting a neutral but cautious policy stance. Although higher oil prices typically support the loonie, the currency is currently trading below its 20-day moving average of 1.4149 as markets grapple with broader Gulf tensions. Investors are now monitoring whether the CAD can reclaim its traditional sensitivity to energy exports or if it will remain suppressed by the BoC's shift away from its previous tightening bias.

sources.title: Dollar near one-week high as markets grapple with Gulf tensions - CNBC
Gold holding steady as Bank of Canada leaves rates unchanged - KITCO

CHF.catalyst: The Swiss franc has experienced a period of relative weakness as risk aversion flows have favored the U.S. dollar following reports of Iranian strikes on military facilities in Jordan and Bahrain. Despite its traditional safe-haven status, the franc is trading above its 20, 50, and 200-day moving averages against the dollar, indicating a sustained period of depreciation in the current environment. Local economic data remains focused on the industrial sector, with VAT Group reporting a record order intake of CHF 500 million for the first half of 2026, driven by semiconductor investment. The currency remains range-bound at the 0.8123 level as traders weigh geopolitical escalations against the Swiss National Bank's current policy path.

sources.title: VAT Media Release on Half-year 2026 Results - TradingView

NZD.catalyst: The New Zealand dollar has surrendered recent gains as geopolitical instability involving Iran offsets the hawkishness of the Reserve Bank of New Zealand. While the RBNZ’s own inflation model for Q2 2026 remains unchanged at 2.7% and the bank maintains a 2.5% hiking stance, speculative positioning remains heavily short with a COT net position of -29,582. The currency is currently showing technical resilience, trading above its 20, 50, and 200-day moving averages, supported by a higher-than-expected CPI print. Market participants are awaiting the next RBNZ inflation data release to see if the central bank will maintain its positive real rate differential over the yen and other low-yielders.

NOK.catalyst: The Norwegian krone remains largely range-bound as it lacks fresh domestic catalysts, though it continues to be influenced by the volatile global energy landscape. The currency is currently trading below its 20-day moving average of 9.778 and its 200-day moving average of 9.736, reflecting a lack of sustained momentum despite the recent climb in crude oil prices. Without specific Norges Bank commentary or significant data surprises in the current session, the krone is tracking broader risk sentiment and the performance of the dollar index. Investors are looking toward the next inflation report to determine if the central bank will deviate from its current policy hold.

SEK.catalyst: The Swedish krona is currently trading in a consolidated state, positioned above its 20, 50, and 200-day moving averages against the dollar. The currency lacks immediate idiosyncratic drivers from the Riksbank today and is primarily moving in sympathy with the euro and broader European sentiment. Market participants are monitoring the 9.704 level as the krona remains sensitive to shifts in global risk appetite and the ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. In the absence of fresh Swedish data, the currency is expected to remain range-bound pending the next scheduled central bank communication or regional inflation print.
